title: Vidukathaiya Intha Vazhkai Song Lyrics Muthu
stanzas:
enadhu kai ennai
adippadhu pol
enadhu viral kannai
keduppathu pol
azhudhu ariyaadha
en kangal aaru
kulamaaga maaruvadho..

yaen endrum
kaetkavum naadhi illai.
aezhaiyin needhikku
kan undu paarvai illai

pasuvinai paambendru
satchi solla mudiyum
kaambinil visham enna
karakkavaa mudiyum

udambil valindhodum
udhiram unai kaetkum
naan seidha
theengu enna
naan seidha
theengu enna

vandhu vilugindra
mazhai thuligal
endha idam saerum
yaar kandaar

mazhai thanil
thondrudhu gangai nadhi
adhu kadal sendru
saervadhu kaala vidhi.

ivanukku ival endru
ezhudhiya kanakku
kanakkugal puriyaamal
kanavukkul vazhakku (2)

kaigalil ponn alli
nee koduthaai.
indru kangalil kanneer
yaen koduthaai.

kaaviyangal unai paada
kaathirukkum pozhudhu
kaavi udai nee kondaal
ennavaagum manadhu.(2)

vazhvai nee thaedi
vadakkae nee ponaai
naangal povadhengae..
naangal povadhengae..

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song?

The song explores themes of longing and the complexities of love, reflecting on the emotional turmoil of the protagonist.

How does this song fit into the movie plot?

In Muthu, this song underscores a pivotal moment of heartbreak, highlighting the protagonist's internal struggles.

Who is the singer and music director of this song?

The song is sung by Hariharan and composed by the renowned AR Rahman.

When was the song released?

Vidukathaiya Intha Vazhkai was released on October 23, 1995.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Vidukathaiya Intha Vazhkai, the protagonist grapples with feelings of love and loss, beautifully conveyed through Hariharan's emotive singing. The song serves as a poignant reflection on the struggles of life and relationships, capturing a melancholic yet romantic essence. Set against the backdrop of the film Muthu, it evokes deep emotions, making it a timeless classic. The haunting melody and heartfelt lyrics resonate with anyone who has faced similar experiences.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Vidukathaiya Intha Vazhkai is a hauntingly beautiful melody that captures the essence of longing and introspection. With Hariharan's soulful vocals and AR Rahman's masterful composition, this song from the film Muthu resonates deeply with anyone who has experienced love and loss. Dive into the emotional depths of this classic that continues to touch hearts even decades after its release.
stanzaMeanings: Who will give an end to this life?
My hands seem to hit me, and my eyes seem to cry without knowing why, will my eyes ever change?
Why is there no one to listen to me? There is no gaze for the beauty of my heart.
The truth can be told as the snake of the cow, but can the poison in the bamboo be removed?
With the body being stretched, what is the pain that I have caused? What is the pain that I have caused?
Who will give an end to this life?
Who has seen where the falling rain drops land?
Who has seen the bonds that people celebrate?
In the rain, the Ganges river starts and merges into the sea, that is the fate of time.
For him, she is counted as a number, without understanding the calculations, the path is in dreams.
The change of relationships and the struggle for rights, when will both be resolved?
Who will give an end to this life?
Your kingdom is not to be mixed with the good ones.
If you are there to do work, it won't be a burden, you are pure.
You gave gold in your hands. Why did you give tears in your eyes today?
When the time comes for the epics to sing, if you wear the epic, what will happen to the heart?
You seek life, but you have gone north, where will we go? Where will we go?
